window_expansion.reason: Strict 72h window (2026-06-27 to 2026-06-30) yielded 2 verified items, below the 5-item threshold

published_items.title: Claude API Rate Limits Unified Across Sonnet, Haiku, and Opus
published_items.notes: Four tiers consolidated to three: Start, Build, Scale. Sonnet and Haiku rate limits now match Opus at every tier.

published_items.title: OpenAI Codex Remote Generally Available on All Paid ChatGPT Plans
published_items.secondary_reason: Primary URL returned HTTP 403. Date confirmed via Releasebot (T3) and URL-pattern dating of TechTimes article (20260627).
published_items.notes: Available on Plus, Pro, Business, Enterprise, Education. Mac/Windows desktop app required for session control. QR-based device pairing.

published_items.title: Claude Generally Available in Microsoft Azure Foundry
published_items.notes: Opus 4.8 and Haiku 4.5 available in East US, West US 2, West Europe. CRITICAL: inference on Anthropic-hosted servers, NOT Azure EU-native. EU data residency not guaranteed. Go/Ruby SDKs not supported at GA.

published_items.title: HP Inc. and OpenAI Launch Frontier Enterprise Partnership
published_items.notes: Feb-May 2026 pilot. Four tracks: CX, telemetry, employee productivity, software dev. 122 PRs and bug-remediation figures are vendor-disclosed only — no independent methodology or baseline.

published_items.title: Anthropic Economic Index: AI Usage Tracks Work Rhythms
published_items.conflict_note: Anthropic measuring own product's workforce impact on self-selected Claude user sample (n≈9700). Not generalisable to broader workforce.
published_items.notes: Key findings: usage mirrors Mon-Fri work schedule with peaks 10am/3pm; ~35% expect AI to handle most work in 12 months; 57% say AI made skills more valuable; women 12% of linked sample.
